channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: These companies help parents try to pick their babies' traits. Experts are wary
item.description: Prospective parents can now pick embryos based on risk predictions for thousands of diseases and odds for specific traits. But should they?
item.content:encoded:
Prospective parents can now pick embryos based on risk predictions for thousands of diseases and odds for specific traits. But should they?

(Image credit: Kate Medley for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Preserving pollinators is good for health -- and income
item.description: Pollinators have economic and health benefits, but those benefits have been difficult to quantify. A new study puts some numbers to how important pollinators are for both nutrition and income.
item.content:encoded: Pollinators have economic and health benefits, but those benefits have been difficult to quantify. A new study puts some numbers to how important pollinators are for both nutrition and income.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Have you lost or are worried about losing SNAP benefits? NPR wants to hear your story
item.description: NPR wants to hear from people impacted by changes to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.
item.content:encoded:
NPR wants to hear from people impacted by changes to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: A new Medicare option for weight loss drugs is coming: Here's what to know
item.description: Millions of people with Medicare will soon be eligible to get discounted GLP-1 drugs for weight loss. Here's how it will work.
item.content:encoded:
Millions of people with Medicare will soon be eligible to get discounted GLP-1 drugs for weight loss. Here's how it will work.

(Image credit: Universal Images Group)
item.dc:creator: Jackie Fortiér

item.title: What to know about the hantavirus outbreak on transatlantic cruise
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Dr. Celine Gounder about the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Dr. Celine Gounder about the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Hantavirus on cruise ship confirmed as rare type that can spread human-to-human
item.description: While most hantaviruses spread through contact with rodent feces, urine or saliva, officials confirm that the type on the cruise ship is Andes hantavirus, which is known to spread between people.
item.content:encoded:
While most hantaviruses spread through contact with rodent feces, urine or saliva, officials confirm that the type on the cruise ship is Andes hantavirus, which is known to spread between people.

(Image credit: Patrick Pleul/picture alliance)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Midwives saved his mom's life -- and inspired him to pursue the profession
item.description: May 5 is International Day of the Midwife. This year's theme is "one million more" — reflecting a shortage of midwives.
item.content:encoded:
May 5 is International Day of the Midwife. This year's theme is "one million more" — reflecting a shortage of midwives.

(Image credit: Ben de la Cruz/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: This flashy group of Portland mall walkers puts neon pep into their step
item.description: Krista Catwood wanted a fun way to exercise, so she started a mall-walking group whose unofficial costume was 1980s garb. The festive, all-ages event has exploded in popularity over the past year.
item.content:encoded:
Krista Catwood wanted a fun way to exercise, so she started a mall-walking group whose unofficial costume was 1980s garb. The festive, all-ages event has exploded in popularity over the past year.

(Image credit: Celeste Noche for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: Anti-poverty program is effective even in one of the world's toughest settings
item.description: Researchers looked at whether a tried-and-true poverty alleviation effort that gives cash and coaching to households, and is typically implemented in rural areas, could work in an urban setting.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers looked at whether a tried-and-true poverty alleviation effort that gives cash and coaching to households, and is typically implemented in rural areas, could work in an urban setting.

(Image credit: Ali Ebubekir Tokcan/Anadolu)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Supreme Court gives abortion pill mifepristone a 1-week reprieve from a major change
item.description: The Supreme Court says rules that allow patients to get the abortion pill mifepristone through the mail can stay the same for at least a week.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court says rules that allow patients to get the abortion pill mifepristone through the mail can stay the same for at least a week.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy
